applied_optimizations.name: nn.GRU baseline with Triton kernels
applied_optimizations.description: Created ModelNew class wrapping nn.GRU with correct interface. Includes working Triton kernels for sigmoid and tanh (using manual tanh implementation for ROCm). Achieves correctness (SCORE 50) but performance is similar to reference due to GPU warmup variance.

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton GRU cell with elementwise fusion
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues with multi-batch indexing in Triton kernels. The sigmoid/tanh elementwise kernels work correctly in isolation, but integrating them into a full GRU cell showed numerical mismatches (max_diff ~0.8, avg_diff ~0.002). The issue appears to be in kernel grid configuration for batched operations - requires more debugging time.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on GRU
attempted_but_failed.reason: torch.compile(mode='default') on nn.GRU resulted in slower performance (0.99x speedup).

notes: Triton on ROCm requires manual tanh: (exp(2x)-1)/(exp(2x)+1) since tl.tanh is unavailable
Triton sigmoid using tl.math.exp works correctly on ROCm
Custom Triton GRU kernel has correctness issues with batched indexing - grid configuration needs careful attention
Simple nn.GRU wrapper achieves correctness (SCORE 50) but speedup varies (0.97x-1.07x) due to GPU warmup effects
Further optimization would require proper torch.profiler profiling to identify specific bottlenecks
